category: brew recipe dose mistaken for retail bag size

consistency.notes: All non-null, non-empty ground-truth labels are supported by the page text. Product title, roaster, location/country, origin, region, process, varieties, producer, altitude, harvest, roast level, tasting notes, producer text, price/currency, default bag size/package grams, and availability all appear explicitly. Boolean inferences are also supported: not a blend (single-origin Ethiopia with one origin listing), not espresso (best enjoyed as filter or gentle immersion brew), not decaf (no decaf indication), not coferment/infused (described as natural process with standard handling), and specialty coffee is supported by the specialty-style single-origin offering with detailed provenance and tasting notes.

realism.reasons: Very plausible as an html-to-text dump of a real specialty roaster product page. The structure matches common Shopify-style coffee pages: breadcrumb, cart/search, title, price, size/grind selectors, stock note, tasting notes, origin details, narrative copy, brew guide, shipping note, related products, variant block, footer. The coffee details are coherent and specific: Chelbesa in Gedeb/Gedeo/Yirgacheffe, Ethiopian varieties 74110/74112, natural process, 1,950–2,200 masl, and a seasonal harvest window all fit specialty-coffee norms. The flavor language is believable and restrained. The processing description and brew guidance sound like real roaster copy, not generic filler. Variant data is also realistic: clean IDs, sensible SKUs, 250g and 1kg sizes, correct availability flags, and a believable price ladder ($22 / $78). Minor tiny tells only: 'North Arcade Coffee Roasters' may or may not be a real brand, and 'default_for_inference' is a bit artifact-like rather than a normal storefront export field. But overall this is close to indistinguishable from a real product-page text rendering.

label.coffee.sensory_text:
Tasting notes: Strawberry jam, bergamot, cocoa nib
In the cup: a high-toned natural with strawberry jam up front, bergamot aromatics, and a clean cocoa-nib finish.
label.coffee.producer_text: This lot comes from smallholder deliveries to Chelbesa Washing Station in Gedeb. Ripe cherries were floated, sorted by hand, and dried slowly on raised beds with careful turning through the hottest parts of the day.
